signals.title: AST SpaceMobile sees New Glenn setback delaying initial commercial service into 2027
signals.summary:
AST SpaceMobile expects Blue Origin’s recent launchpad explosion will delay its direct-to-smartphone constellation by three to six months, investment bank William Blair said in an equity research note, pushing initial commercial services into the first half of 2027. 
 The post  AST SpaceMobile sees New Glenn setback delaying initial commercial service into 2027  appeared first on  SpaceNews .

signals.summary: Google has worked to make its data centers flexible, the company’s global head of data center energy told Utility Dive, but it’s often faster and more cost effective to pay other customers to shift their electricity usage.

signals.title: NASA declares end to MAVEN Mars mission
signals.summary:
NASA has formally ended a Mars mission that has been out of contact for six months while the investigation into the spacecraft’s demise continues. 
 The post  NASA declares end to MAVEN Mars mission  appeared first on  SpaceNews .

signals.title: CrowdStrike narrowly beats estimates on AI tailwinds, but stock falls 10%

signals.summary: President Donald Trump told aides that he won't resume the war unless U.S. troops are killed, according to The Wall Street Journal.

signals.title: NRO nominee says commercial space, AI are reshaping spy satellite agency
signals.summary:
In a confirmation hearing, Roger Mason said clear government demand signals are needed as private investment transforms the space intelligence market 
 The post  NRO nominee says commercial space, AI are reshaping spy satellite agency  appeared first on  SpaceNews .

signals.title: Investors can 'buy' SpaceX early with Coinbase perpetual futures on pre-IPOs
signals.summary: Pre-IPO perps give traders the ability to speculate on the value of a private company without owning shares.

signals.title: Colorado co-op delivers 100% renewables in March, a first
signals.summary: Holy Cross Energy CEO Bryan Hannegan said the utility plans to expand its programs for smart electrification and demand flexibility, and selectively add new flexible renewable resources.

signals.title: Ex-Trump advisor John Bolton agrees to plead guilty to retaining classified information: MS NOW
signals.summary: When he was indicted, John Bolton said he was innocent and that he was being targeted because of his public opposition to President Trump.

signals.title: Lockheed’s GRIZZLY C-UAS system downs attack drone in live-fire demo
signals.summary: The live-fire demonstration paired the radars, Sanctum software and launcher to destroy the target at Yuma Proving Ground, Arizona.

signals.title: Protesters target NV Energy at electric utility conference as anger over affordability rises
signals.summary: “In Las Vegas, one of the fastest warming cities in the country, you cannot live without electricity,” said protest organizer Leslie Vega, who said she’s lost loved ones to heatstroke.

signals.title: Ships stranded in Persian Gulf quietly coordinate with U.S. Navy to exit Hormuz
signals.summary: Clashes erupted between the U.S. and Iran over commercial ships earlier this week.

signals.title: Long-term unemployment is surging in the U.S. There are hidden costs for workers and the economy
signals.summary: Long-term unemployment can have ramifications on financial, emotional and family health that linger even after reentry into the workforce.

signals.title: Sen. Bill Cassidy signs onto brief calling 'anti-weaponization' fund a 'dire threat'
signals.summary: Sen. Bill Cassidy joined an amicus brief calling the Justice Department "anti-weaponization" fund a "dire threat."

signals.title: Slighted market sectors get their time to shine — plus, Costco keeps packing them in
signals.summary: Every weekday, the Investing Club releases the Homestretch; an actionable afternoon update just in time for the last hour of trading.

signals.title: Eli Manning's private equity firm acquires licensing company for NFL Flag in bet on youth sports
signals.summary: Eli Manning's Brand Velocity Group announced a deal to acquire youth sports company RCX amid congressional bill to ban private equity from youth sports.

signals.title: NASA abandons ‘core module’ concept for commercial space station development
signals.summary:
NASA is withdrawing a proposal to revamp its strategy for transitioning from the International Space Station to commercial stations, one that had been criticized by the companies developing such stations. 
 The post  NASA abandons ‘core module’ concept for commercial space station development  appeared first on  SpaceNews .

signals.title: Electric sector needs firm gas supply to protect grid reliability, gas industry report says
signals.summary: The report, prepared for the Natural Gas Council, applauded reforms introduced following Winter Storm Uri in 2021 but said better coordination between the gas and electric sectors is still needed.

signals.title: Speed to power requires more transmission, not less competition
signals.summary: A complaint at FERC seeking to limit competition among transmission developers would inject uncertainty into the process and spur regulatory delays, writes Will Hazelip from National Grid Ventures US.

signals.title: MISO’s resource outlook improves as forecast generation additions outpace demand growth
signals.summary: The Midcontinent Independent System Operator is expected to have growing capacity surpluses over the next five years, according to the OMS-MISO survey.

signals.title: Eli Manning weighs in on Giants QB Jaxson Dart's Trump rally fallout
signals.summary: Eli Manning spoke about political activities from athletes after Giants QB Jaxson Dart introduced President Donald Trump at a rally.

signals.title: AstroForge completes DeepSpace-2 spacecraft
signals.summary:
Asteroid mining startup AstroForge has completed assembly of its latest spacecraft for launch later this year, incorporating lessons from a failed mission last year. 
 The post  AstroForge completes DeepSpace-2 spacecraft  appeared first on  SpaceNews .

signals.title: Delian Asparouhov and Philip Johnston on making the case for orbital data centers
signals.summary:
This episode of Space Minds is from SpaceNews’ recent event on orbital data centers. There, Jeff Foust talks with talks with Founders Fund’s Delian Asparouhov about why he’s warming to […] 
 The post  Delian Asparouhov and Philip Johnston on making the case for orbital data centers  appeared first on  SpaceNews .

signals.title: Hidden chemical weapons sites emerge in Syria amid fragile security transition
signals.summary: Over a decade after Syria agreed to dismantle its arsenal, international inspectors have uncovered scores of previously hidden chemical weapons materials.

signals.title: Dutch plant for combat-zone robots offers fresh supply pipeline for Ukraine
signals.summary: The facility has already produced its first THeMIS vehicle for the Dutch government, part of a run of more than 100 such robots pledged by the Netherlands.

signals.title: Quantinuum stock opens at $68 per share after IPO
signals.summary: Honeywell will retain a majority stake in Quantinuum and will continue to be a strategic customer and partner following the listing.

signals.title: Bitcoin is weathering its ugliest week in months as narrative fades and liquidity rotates
signals.summary: Bitcoin is getting pummeled to kick off June as the market loses its dominant narrative and liquidity continues to rotate into other assets.

signals.title: Confirmed screwworm case in Texas sends two biotech stocks higher
signals.summary: Options volume is surging for Zoetis as traders look for potential stock market winners in light of a screwworm case in Texas.

signals.title: Flesh-eating screwworm is confirmed in the U.S., officials say
signals.summary: The USDA and Texas officials are working to contain New World screwworm from Zavala County, including establishing an infested zone and enforcing quarantine.

signals.title: Vibe-coding phenomenon lifts AI startup Supabase to $10.5 billion valuation
signals.summary: Database startup Supabase announced a $500 million funding round that values the company at $10.5 billion, including the fresh capital.

signals.title: Inside the $850 million Obama Presidential Center ahead of its Juneteenth opening
signals.summary: President Obama's library will cost $30 to visit, making it the most expensive presidential museum or library in modern history.

signals.title: [13F] Scion Asset Mgmt (Burry) — nuovo filing 2023-08-10
signals.summary: Controlla il filing per vedere le nuove posizioni aperte/aumentate.

signals.title: 7 states sue Trump administration over TotalEnergies offshore wind lease buyout
signals.summary: The lawsuit calls the deal a “sham settlement agreement to unlawfully cancel an offshore wind lease and redirect the money paid for the lease to a separate, unauthorized use favored by the President.”

signals.title: China builds institutional framework for space computing push
signals.summary:
China is establishing an industrial policy framework to support a push to build space-based computing infrastructure, with the emergence of influential coordinating bodies. 
 The post  China builds institutional framework for space computing push  appeared first on  SpaceNews .

signals.title: Rheinmetall sells struggling auto division to focus on defense
signals.summary: The unit will be sold to Aequita for a provisional €350 million, subject to potential adjustment when the deal closes, expected in the fourth quarter.

signals.title: Blackstone restricts flagship fund withdrawals as private asset fears reemerge
signals.summary: Blackstone capped withdrawals from its $79 billion BCRED fund after redemption requests rose from 7.9% to 10%, adding to private market liquidity concerns.

signals.title: As the largest World Cup ever kicks off, health officials are focused on more than Ebola
signals.summary: Officials are more concerned about highly contagious diseases like measles and respiratory viruses, which can spread quickly through large, fast-moving crowds.

signals.title: Soaring stocks created 2 million new millionaires around the world last year
signals.summary: The population of global millionaires surged 7.9% to 25.3 million in 2025, according to the Capgemini World Wealth Report.

signals.title: Customer experience, better modeling can boost demand-side portfolio: report
signals.summary: The Brattle Group’s report lays out a framework for increasing demand-side resources to mitigate the impacts of load growth, variable renewables and distributed electrification.

signals.title: DTE Energy partners with LG to deploy 6 GWh of battery storage
signals.summary: By 2042, DTE expects to have more than 2.9 GW of energy storage on its system, more than doubling its current storage capacity.

signals.title: Experts warn terrorism threat is rising in Africa as US pulls back
signals.summary: A jihadist front is gaining ground across Africa, the Center for Strategic and International Studies warned in its annual risk analysis.

signals.title: Elon Musk's net worth poised to sail past $1 trillion in SpaceX IPO
signals.summary: Based on SpaceX's updated IPO prospectus, Elon Musk owns shares in the company worth more than $866 billion.

signals.title: Kalshi is building a prediction markets 'Bloomberg Terminal' for high-end traders, source says
signals.summary: The prediction market platform is developing the platform to create a singular platform for its highly engaged traders.

signals.title: SpaceX IPO hype has traders flocking to this mid-cap stock
signals.summary: Options volume is picking up in Nasdaq-listed Echostar, a $35-billion networking business based in Colorado that owns an estimated 3% of SpaceX stock.

signals.title: Higher mortgage rates don't just keep buyers on the sidelines. Application denials rise too
signals.summary: The denial rate in loan applications was 15.1% in 2024, up from 12.2% in 2021, a rise that occurred alongside surging mortgage rates, the St. Louis Fed found.

signals.title: Tire giant Pirelli vows legal action as short seller alleges ‘close relationship’ with Russia
signals.summary: Pirelli has instructed lawyers to take legal action after short seller Grizzly Research claimed the firm has a "close relationship" with Russia.

signals.title: SoftBank shares plunge over 11% amid broader tech sell-off
signals.summary: Shares of SoftBank fell 11% following an overnight sell-off in the U.S. market on the back of wider profit taking in the tech sector.

signals.title: SpaceX targets $135 IPO price at valuation of $1.77 trillion
signals.summary: The valuation would make Elon Musk's company the seventh-biggest in the U.S., above Tesla, which has a market cap of about $1.6 trillion.

signals.title: Bitcoin's high-conviction holders are turning into sellers as the crypto's price hits new lows
signals.summary: In the past two days, bitcoin's highest-conviction holders have sold about $2.4 billion in bitcoin.

signals.title: Pentagon chief sounds ‘alarm’ over China’s buildup, urges allies to boost defense spending
signals.summary: Hegseth urged Asian allies to ramp up military spending to counter China’s growing power.

signals.title: After cooperation on SMILE mission, ESA and China chart parallel but separate paths
signals.summary:
ESA and China recently launched the joint SMILE magnetosphere mission after a decade of cooperation, but despite similar goals, another collaboration appears distant. 
 The post  After cooperation on SMILE mission, ESA and China chart parallel but separate paths  appeared first on  SpaceNews .

signals.title: Electronics manufacturer Murata to explore Xona satellite timing service for telecom, data centers
signals.summary:
Xona is developing a low Earth orbit network to provide backup to GPS-dependent systems 
 The post  Electronics manufacturer Murata to explore Xona satellite timing service for telecom, data centers  appeared first on  SpaceNews .

signals.title: Europe is rearming together — except in space
signals.summary:
Europe is rapidly rewriting its security architecture. Faced with Russian aggression, mounting doubts about long-term American commitment and growing pressure to shoulder more of its own defense burden, European states […] 
 The post  Europe is rearming together — except in space  appeared first on  SpaceNews .

signals.title: Muon Space unveils Starship-class satellite platform for orbital data centers
signals.summary:
Muon Space announced a Starship-class satellite platform June 3 designed from the ground up to meet the demands of the emerging orbital data center market, with an initial launch slated for 2028 after securing customers. 
 The post  Muon Space unveils Starship-class satellite platform for orbital data centers  appeared first on  SpaceNews .

signals.title: Blue Origin seeks to resume New Glenn launches by year’s end
signals.summary:
Blue Origin’s CEO says damage to its New Glenn launch pad is not as bad as feared and the rocket could return to flight by the end of the year. 
 The post  Blue Origin seeks to resume New Glenn launches by year’s end  appeared first on  SpaceNews .

signals.title: US tells Europe, Canada to boost NATO air and naval forces
signals.summary: “Nations just need to assign the capabilities they have to NATO,” a spokesman for the alliance's military headquarters said.

signals.title: Gulf tensions escalate as Iran hits Kuwait, US strikes near Hormuz
signals.summary: CENTCOM said it had carried out a new round of defensive strikes in southern Iran, while an Iranian drone attack on the Kuwait airport killed one person.

signals.title: At a NATO range in Latvia, hits and misses mark Europe’s counter-drone journey
signals.summary: Drone technology is “a couple of steps ahead” of countermeasures, one officer said, with interceptors having to work every time to prevent losses.

signals.title: The US military wants to showcase battle-ready laser weapons by 2028
signals.summary: A directed energy demonstration is expected to occur during the summer of 2028, as part of a series of planned Golden Dome-related events.

signals.title: Global SSA Market to Reach $61B as Governments Prioritize Space Security, Resilience, and Orbital Safety
signals.summary:
Paris, France – May 2026 – The 2nd edition of Novaspace’s Space Situational Awareness (SSA) report points to a pivotal decade ahead, with cumulative global spending projected to reach $61 […] 
 The post  Global SSA Market to Reach $61B as Governments Prioritize Space Security, Resilience, and Orbital Safety  appeared first on  SpaceNews .

signals.title: The importance of determining an equilibrium state for space traffic management
signals.summary:
The rapid expansion of human and robotic activity in low Earth orbit makes critically important the need to implement effective space traffic management (STM). This will be one of the […] 
 The post  The importance of determining an equilibrium state for space traffic management  appeared first on  SpaceNews .

signals.title: UK explores Vast space station mission for astronaut with physical disability
signals.summary:
The U.K. government is exploring sending British astronaut John McFall to Vast’s planned Haven-1 space station in a mission it says could make him the first person with a physical disability to live in orbit. 
 The post  UK explores Vast space station mission for astronaut with physical disability  appeared first on  SpaceNews .

signals.title: Impulse Space raises $500 million
signals.summary:
Impulse Space has raised $500 million to expand production of orbital transfer vehicles and other spacecraft to support growing commercial and government demand. 
 The post  Impulse Space raises $500 million  appeared first on  SpaceNews .

signals.title: Voyager to acquire lunar lander developer Astrobotic
signals.summary:
Voyager Technologies will acquire Astrobotic, a company developing lunar landers and reusable suborbital vehicles, in a deal worth up to $300 million. 
 The post  Voyager to acquire lunar lander developer Astrobotic  appeared first on  SpaceNews .

signals.title: Researchers call for regulations to protect low Earth orbit environment
signals.summary:
BOULDER, Colorado — Researchers are calling for increased attention and better protection against the rising introduction of exotic materials into Earth’s atmosphere from satellite and space hardware re-entry, especially in […] 
 The post  Researchers call for regulations to protect low Earth orbit environment  appeared first on  SpaceNews .

signals.title: France to fly two astronauts on Vast missions
signals.summary:
Commercial space station developer Vast has reached an agreement with the French government to fly two French astronauts on its missions, including the first flight to its Haven-1 space station. 
 The post  France to fly two astronauts on Vast missions  appeared first on  SpaceNews .

signals.title: Future presence of US troops in Lithuania is ‘under review,’ says minister
signals.summary: U.S. troops presently stationed in the country are now leaving as expected, but the next scheduled group is not arriving, the defense minister said.

signals.title: Celebrating 40 years of Defense News
signals.summary: Since 1986, Defense News has been the authoritative voice for the world's defense decision-makers.

signals.title: France restricts Israeli presence at Europe’s biggest defense show
signals.summary: The restrictions come amid strained relations between Paris and Jerusalem over Israel’s military operations in Lebanon.

signals.title: US, NATO allies to launch scaled-back Baltic Sea drills
signals.summary: Officials say the smaller footprint reflects operational realities rather than waning commitment.

signals.title: US Navy selects companies for at-sea MUSV prototype testing
signals.summary: The U.S. Navy announced on Friday that it had selected seven companies to compete to secure the service’s Medium Unmanned Surface Vessel contract.

signals.title: US may consider placing nukes in Poland, Baltic States, report says
signals.summary: While the discussions were ongoing, an agreement to expand U.S. nuclear hosting was not imminent, an unnamed source told the FT.

signals.title: NATO in Hormuz is not mission creep
signals.summary: "For much of the last three decades, NATO has operated on the assumption that allied security cannot be defined solely by geography."
